title: garnotia acutigluma
constituents: Garnotia acutigluma has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, saponins, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, thereby improving symptoms of conditions like arthritis, eczema, and infections. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Garnotia acutigluma may also help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and certain cancers by improving cardiovascular health and boosting the immune system. Furthermore, the sesquiterpenes in the plant have been found to possess antiparasitic properties, making it a potential treatment for diseases like malaria and other parasitic infections.
side_effects: Garnotia acutigluma can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high levels of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to its volatile oils can lead to respiratory problems, including shortness of breath and bronchitis. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or flowers can cause stomach pain, vomiting, and diarrhea, while skin contact may result in irritation, redness, and inflammation. If not handled carefully, garnotia acutigluma can also cause allergic reactions, including hives and anaphylaxis in severe cases.
